name: Frontend Engineer Rubric
audience: frontend engineers who need stronger recruiter-readable proof
weeklyTheme: Tighten usable public proof for core frontend roles.

signals.label: Target frontend role is explicit
signals.gap: The profile summary does not state the frontend target clearly enough.
signals.nextAction: Rewrite the summary so the intended frontend role is visible immediately.

signals.label: Enough public projects exist
signals.gap: The portfolio is still too light for strong recruiter confidence.
signals.nextAction: List at least three public proofs with clear project names and outcomes.

signals.label: GitHub is linked and usable as proof
signals.gap: No GitHub link exists for recruiter review.
signals.nextAction: Add a public GitHub link and make the pinned repos easy to scan.

signals.label: Portfolio surface exists
signals.gap: A portfolio or case-study surface is missing.
signals.nextAction: Link a portfolio and explain what each project proves.

signals.label: Frontend patterns are visible in proof
signals.gap: The proof list does not yet show the patterns most frontend roles screen for.
signals.nextAction: Add or rename proof artifacts to show dashboards, design systems, forms, accessibility, or performance work.

signals.label: Core frontend depth is visible
signals.gap: The skill section is not yet strong enough to support the role story.
signals.nextAction: List the strongest frontend skills already proven by real work.

signals.label: Interview narratives exist
signals.gap: There is not yet enough visible narrative to support interview explanations.
signals.nextAction: Draft stories around state management, testing, or performance tradeoffs.

signals.label: Truthfulness and scope boundaries are explicit
signals.gap: The profile does not make its truthfulness and project-scope boundaries explicit.
signals.nextAction: Add one or two constraints that prevent overclaiming or vague project descriptions.

projectBlueprints.title: Product Dashboard Case Study
projectBlueprints.problem: Many frontend repos show code but not the product problem or usability tradeoffs.
projectBlueprints.solution: Turn an existing dashboard project into a documented case study with screenshots, decisions, and accessibility notes.
projectBlueprints.recruiterTakeaway: Shows product thinking and maintainable frontend execution.

readmeChecklist: State the user problem and what the project proves.
Show screenshots in the first screen.
truthfulnessGuardrails: Do not claim scale or adoption without evidence.
Only state decisions you actually made.
positioningComparison: Use occupation-ops to tighten public proof before sending more applications.
Use job-search operations tools after the proof surfaces are ready to share.
